item.title: U.S. military launches second night of strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. military launched another round of strikes against Iran late Wednesday night, U.S. Central Command said, in the second night of attacks as diplomacy between the two countries appears to collapse.

item.title: U.S. launches more strikes against Iran after Trump says ceasefire is "over"
item.description: The U.S. began a second night of strikes against Iranian targets late Wednesday, following Iranian attacks on commercial oil tankers.

item.title: Person of interest in custody after stray bullet hits 5-year-old in Detroit
item.description: Kaizer, 5, is expected to make a full recovery after a stray bullet struck him while he rode his bicycle near his west side Detroit home Tuesday evening, his family said.

item.title: U.S. launches another round of strikes against Iran, CENTCOM says
item.description: U.S. Central Command says it has begun conducting additional strikes against Iran in order to hold Iran "accountable" for "recent unjustified aggression." CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Dow closes nearly 600 points down amid renewed U.S.-Iran fighting
item.description: The Dow Jones tumbled on Wednesday, closing down nearly 600 points as oil prices rose after President Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran "over." MarketWatch Washington bureau chief Rob Schroeder joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump hails "unity" at NATO summit, despite recent tensions
item.description: President Trump concluded the NATO summit with a press conference touting the "tremendous love" exhibited by the allies.

item.title: Graham Platner expected to release pre-recorded video on campaign's future, source says
item.description: Maine Democrats are calling on Senate nominee Graham Platner to drop out of the race ahead of a key deadline after a woman accused him of sexual assault. Platner denies the allegation. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has the latest.

item.title: Hannah Graham: Deadly Connections
item.description: "48 Hours" goes inside the murder investigation of the UVA student, the evidence that links one man to three horrific crimes, and now, his punishment

item.title: Hannah Graham:Stalked by Evil
item.description: "48 Hours" goes inside the investigation of the death of the UVA student and the evidence that links her alleged killer to other crimes spanning a decade

item.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Sons of man killed by ICE officer in Houston demand independent probe
item.description: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo worked for 35 years to send all three of his American citizen sons to college, his son said. He was shot and killed by an ICE officer on Tuesday.

item.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
item.description: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

item.title: How much interest can an $18,000 high-yield savings account earn this year?
item.description: Have $18,000 in a traditional savings account? Here's the interest you could be earning with a high-yield account.

item.title: How much interest can you earn with a 4.20% CD account now?
item.description: It's easy to find a CD account with an interest rate of 4.20% now. Here's how much interest that translates to.

item.title: Watch: JD Vance takes questions on Iran, 2020 election, more
item.description: Vice President JD Vance slammed Iran at a press conference in Milwaukee Wednesday, saying that if they shoot at ships, the U.S. will "knock the hell out of them." Mr. Vance also bashed Democrats over Graham Platner's sexual assault allegations. Lindsey Reiser reports.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will give Ukraine right to make Patriot missiles
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that the U.S. will grant Ukraine's request for a production license to build Patriot missile interceptors for defense. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $10,000 18-month CD will earn savers who act now
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD of this size and length will be substantial, but that's not the only benefit for savers.

item.title: Parents of football player who died speak out after charges filed against ex-coach
item.description: Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday announced that his office is charging former Bucknell coach Mark Kulbis with felony aggravated hazing.

item.title: DOGE shuts down after controversial run with Elon Musk
item.description: DOGE, the government agency once led by Elon Musk which canceled billions in public contracts and sent scores of federal employees packing, is shutting down. CBS News Mornings anchor Michael George reports.

item.title: Judge orders release of over $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump case
item.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

item.title: Sneak peek: My Mother's Murder Trials
item.description: ENCORE: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports in a two-part "48 Hours" airing Saturday, July 11 from 9-11 p.m. ET/PT on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Ex-Bucknell University coach charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: A former Bucknell University coach has been charged in the death of a freshman football player who collapsed during practice and died in 2024.

item.title: 7/7: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: More Democrats call for Graham Platner to end his campaign; U.S. launches new strikes against Iran over attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel expected to say Israel needs "significant changes" in speech
item.description: Rahm Emanuel​ is viewed as a potential Democratic presidential candidate and has been a longtime defender of Israel.

item.title: Walmart cuts prices on thousands of items, including beef and soda
item.description: President Trump praised Walmart and said the lower prices are the result of "my Administration's request to celebrate our great Country's 250th birthday."

item.title: Starter Homes are a Million Bucks Now | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
Starter homes now cost $1 million in many parts of the country, leaving younger generations wondering whether homeownership is still a realistic goal. Jill asks whether buying a house should remain a cornerstone of the American dream, or if the idea itself has become outdated. Plus, caller questions: Dorian is feeling the financial strain of wedding season as travel costs, gifts and pricey trips pile up. And Andrew, a new dad, wants to know whether now is the right time to buy term life insurance.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: Don't Buy a House Together Until You Hear This | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
Caller Aaron has a family mess on his hands: his cousin bought a house with a girlfriend; they broke up, and now his cousin's credit is still tied to the property while the ex continues living there and refuses to refinance. Jill explains exactly what to do before buying a major asset with someone you're not married to. Plus, Jill breaks down the new student loan rules that took effect on July 1, 2026, how parents and prospective students should navigate the new rules and why taking on debt for college may not be worth it anymore.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: Boomer Parents: It's Time to Cough it Up | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
Americans have been anxiously awaiting the so-called "great wealth transfer," where trillions of dollars are expected to pass down from Baby Boomers to their kids. But people are living longer and holding onto their assets. Jill says it's time to start opening up the purse strings now. Plus, caller questions: Toby asks whether sterling silver jewelry is actually a worthwhile investment, and Dave wants to know if taking a pay cut to improve his quality of life is financially realistic.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say her Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.
